full_response:
For the operator  

P_{ab}=g_{ab}+U_{a}U_{b}

all the nice properties found in part (a) (symmetry, idempotence, trace = 3 and, most importantly, P_{ab}U^{b}=0) rely on U^{a} being a unit 4-velocity, i.e.  

U^{a}U_{a}=-1 .

If U^{a} is just any timelike vector, |U^{a}U_{a}|≠1, the term U_{a}U_{b} must be rescaled so that the contraction with U^{b} still vanishes.  Write the squared norm

U^{2}\equiv g_{cd}U^{c}U^{d}<0 .

Demanding P_{ab}U^{b}=0 gives

0=(g_{ab}+A\,U_{a}U_{b})U^{b}=U_{a}+A\,U_{a}U^{2}
            ⟹ A=-\frac{1}{U^{2}} .

Therefore the correct projection operator for an arbitrary timelike vector U^{a} is  

P_{ab}=g_{ab}-\frac{U_{a}U_{b}}{U^{c}U_{c}},      or equivalently      P^{a}{}_{b}=δ^{a}{}_{b}-\frac{U^{a}U_{b}}{U^{c}U_{c}} .

With this choice one checks:

•  P_{ab}U^{b}=0  (orthogonality)  
•  P^{a}{}_{c}P^{c}{}_{b}=P^{a}{}_{b}  (idempotent)  
•  P^{a}{}_{a}=3  (projects onto a 3-dimensional subspace)  

and when U^{a} is already unit (U^{2}=-1) the formula reduces to the original one.

\boxed{\,P^{a}{}_{b}=δ^{a}{}_{b}-\dfrac{U^{a}U_{b}}{U^{c}U_{c}}\,}

Confidence: 88%
